name: Elephant Stew
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the elephant into bite-size pieces. This process may take approximately 2 months.
2. Reserve the trunk for later use, as it can be used to store the pieces.
3. Place the elephant pieces in a large pot that is big enough to hold them all.
4. Add sufficient brown gravy to cover the pieces.
5. Cook over a kerosene fire at 450 degrees Fahrenheit until the meat reaches a golden brown color. This process may take about 4 weeks.
6. The recipe serves approximately 3,800 people. If more guests are expected, you can add 2 whole rabbits (optional). However, this should be done only if absolutely necessary, as most people do not enjoy finding rabbit in their stew.
